decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing thermal resistance and lowering the peak temperature. This is the primary geometric lever now that conductivity is maxed out.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved heat flux diversion towards the side boundaries
risk_notes: Ensure the expanded width (0.84) does not cause overlap issues if other components were to move later (currently safe within 1.2m domain)
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader extends too far beyond the heat source relative to the boundary conditions
If this single change is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to combine with base plate conductivity increases
